user_prompt: The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect.
build error, my home computer builds the project fine
recently we tweaked the sdkconfig stuff inside `platformio.ini` enough so that we have enough IRAM to start using PSRAM again, we did that because PNG decoding was failing to allocate enough heap.
agent_reply: Fixed. The build error was a malformed generated command: this machine’s PlatformIO ESP-IDF builder was producing `python.exepython.exe`, so Windows rejected it as an invalid filename.
- Added PSRAM/16 MB local board config: [boards/thefly-core2.json](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/boards/thefly-core2.json:1)
- Pointed `[env:thefly]` at that board and pinned IDF flash size to 16 MB: [platformio.ini](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:41)
- Added a small post-build env fix for duplicated `ESPIDF_PYTHONEXE`: [tools/fix_espidf_pythonexe.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/fix_espidf_pythonexe.py:1)
I also removed the ignored stale local `sdkconfig.thefly*` files so they regenerated from the corrected 16 MB defaults.

user_prompt: can you explain `M5GFX` and particularly the usage of `waitDisplay()` and `display()`? Is it a buffered mode? We are not using it, correct?
agent_reply: Yes: we use **M5GFX**, but we are **not using `waitDisplay()` / `.display()` as a buffered-present render model** in our GUI.
`M5GFX` is M5Stack’s wrapper around LovyanGFX. In our app, [src/main.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:88) sets:
and [src/all_init.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:107) initializes it through `M5.begin(cfg)`, then sets brightness/color depth and clears the screen at lines 115-117.

For our target, [platformio.ini](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:73) uses `thefly-core2`, with `ARDUINO_M5STACK_Core2` defined in [boards/thefly-core2.json](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/boards/thefly-core2.json:8). M5GFX maps that to `Panel_M5StackCore2`, which inherits `Panel_ILI9342`, which inherits `Panel_LCD`.
- `.display()` falls back to `Panel_Device::display()`, which just calls `_bus->flush()`: `.pio/.../Panel_Device.cpp:101`
- and on ESP32 SPI, `Bus_SPI::flush()` is also empty in this version: `.pio/.../Bus_SPI.hpp:125`

So on our Core2 LCD path, drawing is basically **immediate-mode streaming over SPI into the LCD controller’s GRAM**. Calls like `fillRect`, `drawString`, and `pushImage` send pixels directly. There is no app-side full framebuffer that needs `.display()` to “present.”
The `SpinTile` example uses this generic pattern:

That pattern matters for panels with explicit refresh behavior, like e-paper, OLED/framebuffer-backed panels, HDMI-ish backends, or cache-writeback panels. It means “wait until prior display update is done, then request/flush the next one.” For our LCD, it collapses to “do nothing special.”

I also grepped `src`, `lib`, and `inc`: no calls to `waitDisplay`, `setAutoDisplay`, `thefly_display.display`, `M5.Display.display`, `M5Canvas`, or `LGFX_Sprite`. The `FlyGui::display()` method is only an accessor returning `thefly_display`, not the M5GFX present call.

and [src/all_init.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:107) initializes it through `M5.begin(cfg)`, then sets brightness/color depth and clears the screen at lines 115-117.

For our target, [platformio.ini](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:73) uses `thefly-core2`, with `ARDUINO_M5STACK_Core2` defined in [boards/thefly-core2.json](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/boards/thefly-core2.json:8). M5GFX maps that to `Panel_M5StackCore2`, which inherits `Panel_ILI9342`, which inherits `Panel_LCD`.
- `.display()` falls back to `Panel_Device::display()`, which just calls `_bus->flush()`: `.pio/.../Panel_Device.cpp:101`
- and on ESP32 SPI, `Bus_SPI::flush()` is also empty in this version: `.pio/.../Bus_SPI.hpp:125`

The `SpinTile` example uses this generic pattern:

That pattern matters for panels with explicit refresh behavior, like e-paper, OLED/framebuffer-backed panels, HDMI-ish backends, or cache-writeback panels. It means “wait until prior display update is done, then request/flush the next one.” For our LCD, it collapses to “do nothing special.”

I also grepped `src`, `lib`, and `inc`: no calls to `waitDisplay`, `setAutoDisplay`, `thefly_display.display`, `M5.Display.display`, `M5Canvas`, or `LGFX_Sprite`. The `FlyGui::display()` method is only an accessor returning `thefly_display`, not the M5GFX present call.
